entry.title: Where can sports clubs start learning how to use SAP Analytics Cloud system to analyze data?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring SAP Analytics Cloud for Sport Industry learning journey


Overview
Sports clubs around the world are under tremendous pressure to succeed. With SAP Sports One, sports clubs can optimize their operations in training management, scouting, performance diagnostics, match analysis, and many other areas. Additionally, sports clubs are also implementing SAP Analytics Cloud. The analytics tool allows sports clubs to analyze and present their collected data in the best possible way. Through this, the club can gain important insights to drive sustainable growth.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, you will learn how to connect, manage, and customize your SAP Analytics Cloud system. You will also be able to create your first basic SAC story. In addition, you will learn how to integrate and adjust the SAP Sports One Reference Content.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge.
 
Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Transportation Order Generic Request Out: get empty Package Items
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

According to customer specifications, empty packs sometimes have to be sent along in a handling unit. In the TM module, however, these items are filtered out for the ASN. This happens exactly at this point:

/SCMTMS/CL_TOR_GEN_REQ_OUT_SRV->FILTER_CONSIGNEE_ITEMS

 

 LOOP AT it_item_all ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_item>)
    WHERE consignee_id = iv_consignee.

    INSERT <ls_item> INTO TABLE et_item_filtered.

  ENDLOOP.

 



These items are not transferred to the table because no recipient is defined. My idea is to adjust this with an enhancement point so that I can transfer the pack items without recipients to the table. However, I don't know whether unwanted items are transferred to the table as a result. Can anyone give me any tips or suggest another solution?

Is it something missing still.

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YM], %Year%)

IF MONTH([d/Date]) <= @Period THEN
DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Actual")

entry.title: Error in hdblcm while adding the first worker host to a SAP HANA system on Google Cloud
entry.content:
Adding a worker host with hdblcm to a SAP HANA system on Google Cloud is failing.
The primary host is 'hana-mh-a', the worker host to be added is 'hana-mh-aw1'. It is the first and only worker.

This is the error in the hdblcm log (more of the log further below):

Cannot verify password: sapstartsrv of host 'hana-mh-a' is not running
Cannot establish HTTPS connection to 'hana-mh-a': Net::HTTPS: connect: timeout

However, on the primary, I can see that sapstartsrv is indeed running:

[root@hana-mh-a ~]# ps auwx | grep sapstartsrv
sapadm 97216 0.0 0.0 708680 78900 ? Ssl 05:29 0:19 /usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/sapstartsrv pf=/usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/host_profile -D
mh1adm 145567 0.0 0.0 434136 57876 ? Ssl 08:36 0:00 /usr/sap/MH1/HDB50/exe/sapstartsrv pf=/usr/sap/MH1/SYS/profile/MH1_HDB50_hana-mh-a

I also tried starting and stopping sapstartsrv, both the database and the hostctrl instances:

sapcontrol -nr 50 -function StopService MH1
sapcontrol -nr 50 -function StartService MH1
service saphostagent.service restart

Key exchange between the servers has been successful:

[root@hana-mh-a ~]# cat ~/.ssh/authorized_keys
ssh-rsa AA...ws= root@hana-mh-a
ssh-rsa AA...ws= root@hana-mh-a
ssh-rsa AA...nk= root@hana-mh-aw1

[root@hana-mh-aw1 ~]# cat ~/.ssh/authorized_keys
ssh-rsa AA...nk= root@hana-mh-aw1
ssh-rsa AA...nk= root@hana-mh-aw1
# Added by Google
ssh-rsa AA...ws= root@hana-mh-a

On the primary, this is the hdblcm command that is failing:

cat passwd.xml | ./hdblcm --action=add_hosts --addhosts="hana-mh-aw1" --root_user=root --listen_interface=global --read_password_from_stdin=xml -b

The system was set up with Google Cloud's Terraform script. The passwords in passwd.xml are those provided to the Terraform script and have independently been validated on the primary.

Why would the error be with an HTTPS connection to the primary given that I am running the hdblcm command from the primary? Is this message originating from a script being run from the worker?
Any advice for what could be going wrong and how to fix it?

More of the hdblcm error log:

INFO: Starting external program /hana/shared/MH1/global/hdb/install/bin/hdbmodify
INFO: Command line is: /hana/shared/MH1/global/hdb/install/bin/hdbmodify -b --read_password_from_stdin=xml --instlog_dir=/var/tmp/hdb_MH1_hdblcm_add_hosts_2024-10-21_08.06.25 --nostart=off --skip_modify_sudoers=off --sid=MH1 --sapmnt=/hana/shared --install_hostagent=on --auto_initialize_services=on --root_user=root --addhosts=hana-mh-aw1:role=worker:group=default --remote_execution=ssh --use_non_shared_storage=true
INFO: Output line 1:
INFO: Output line 2:
INFO: Output line 3: SAP HANA Lifecycle Management - hdbmodify_addhosts 2.7.72
INFO: Output line 4: **********************************************************
INFO: Output line 5:
INFO: Output line 6:
INFO: Output line 7: System Properties:
INFO: Output line 8: MH1 /hana/shared/MH1 HDB_ALONE
INFO: Output line 9: HDB50
INFO: Output line 10: version: 2.00.079.0
INFO: Output line 11: host: hana-mh-a (Database Worker (worker))
INFO: Output line 12: edition: SAP HANA Database
INFO: Output line 13:
INFO: Output line 14: Start reading from input channel...
INFO: Output line 15: ... Done.
INFO: Output line 16: Modifying configuration of SAP HANA System 'MH1'...
INFO: Output line 17:
INFO: Output line 18: Summary before execution:
INFO: Output line 19: Installation Path: /hana/shared
INFO: Output line 20: SAP HANA System ID: MH1
INFO: Output line 21: Additional Hosts: hana-mh-aw1:role=worker:group=default
INFO: Output line 22: Enable the installation or upgrade of the SAP Host Agent: Yes
INFO: Output line 23: Remote Execution: ssh
INFO: Output line 24: Root User Name For Remote Hosts: root
INFO: Output line 25:
INFO: Output line 26: Adding additional host...
INFO: Output line 27: Adding host 'hana-mh-aw1'...
INFO: Output line 28: hana-mh-aw1: Running in batch mode
INFO: Output line 29: Reconfiguring SAP HANA system failed!
INFO: Output line 30: Add hosts:
INFO: Output line 31: Creating a multiple host system failed
INFO: Output line 32: Adding host 'hana-mh-aw1' failed!
INFO: Output line 33: Add host failed
INFO: Output line 34: Running in batch mode
INFO: Output line 35: Cannot verify password: sapstartsrv of host 'hana-mh-a' is not running
INFO: Output line 36: Cannot establish HTTPS connection to 'hana-mh-a': Net::HTTPS: connect: timeout

entry.title: Kickstart your Career: Become an SAP-Certified Solution Transformation Consultant
entry.content:
In a fast-paced world where companies rely on adaptation and transformation to have success, the role of the solution transformation consultant takes center stage. SAP Solution Transformation Consultants are responsible for guiding the entire business transformation journey, from initial design to final deployment, using a comprehensive end-to-end toolchain. They play a crucial role in accelerating transformation projects by leveraging best practices and ensuring high-quality business outcomes.

They navigate through digital landscapes and guide customers on a transformative journey from traditional ERP systems to the innovative SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Envision playing a central role in the RISE with SAP initiative, empowering enterprises to surge ahead in their market spaces. And with this brand-new role-based certification, we are helping you to achieve this goal.

Are you ready to shape the future of business transformation?

Check out our brand-new role-based certification SAP Certified Associate - Solution Transformation Consultant with SAP Cloud ALM. This certification validates that you possess the application lifecycle management skills and core knowledge in SAP Cloud ALM required for the consultant profile. By getting certified, you demonstrate that you have a comprehensive understanding, and the in-depth skills required to lead a project team as a solution transformation consultant, guiding the team through the solution transformation journey using SAP Cloud ALM. 

Start with this free learning journey

To get started, take the new free, self-paced learning journey "Transforming for Success: Solution Transformation with SAP Cloud ALM" on the SAP Learning site.  In just 16 hours – divided into six sections– you will learn how SAP Cloud ALM, as part of SAP's transformation approach, supports the entire solution transformation journey—from business and solution transformation methodology to agile implementation and efficient operations. After completing this learning journey, you will have the skills to leverage SAP Cloud ALM for comprehensive solution transformation and have an overview of SAP's business transformation strategy.

If you want to learn more about how SAP can help you accelerate digital transformation, check out the story of Martina Ohlerth and benefit from her first-hand experience!

Certified learners need to continuously keep their skills up-to-date and certifications valid by completing mandatory assessments before their certification expires. With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can prepare for and take the stay certified assessments at any time during the validity period of your certification.  If you want to know more on how to get and stay certified, you can read this blog post.

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and get inspired by reading learner stories on SAP Community, ask questions and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: Bugtoberfest Wrap Up!
entry.content:
Posting on @MichelleMoudy 's behalf.

Bugtoberfest is officially over! I hope everyone who wanted to participate was able to complete the tutorials and find all the bugs! As the week is officially over, those of us who wrote these bug hunts wanted to share the intended bugs we wrote into them so y'all had an opportunity to check your work.

But before getting to that, I wanted to share a bit about the origin of the name bug used for coding errors. While most believe the origin comes from a real moth getting comfy in a relay in the Mark II at Harvard in 1947, the word actually comes from the terms bugbear or bugaboo. The term bug as used to describe an issue or error in engineering and can be seen in use by Thomas Edison in 1878, well before it was used to describe software or hardware faults.

As a fun aside, us developer advocates had a debate to the origin of the term at a dinner during TechEd Virtual and I ruined many evenings by sharing this fun fact with everyone. I even had to read aloud an article to prove that it didn't originate from the Harvard moth (which was shared as folks found it really funny a bug was caused by a real bug), as I was met with such disbelief. As a result, I was told I had to include this "fun" fact and story somewhere in relation to Bugtoberfest. 

For proof that I am in fact correct on the origin of the term bug, see here for more details and links to sources.

Now that everyone is aware of the origin of bugs, check out each of the sections below to see the bugs we purposefully wrote into our individual bug hunts. 

ABAP

Easy

 

 

 

CLASS ztest
 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS test_value.
    METHODS test_value1.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ztest
 IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD test_value2.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD test_value1
  ENDCLASS.

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 17, missing full stop `.` after `METHOD test_value1`.
Bug 2: At line 18, missing `ENDMETHOD` for `test_value1 METHOD`.
Bug 3: At line 15, implemented Wrong Method `test_value2` instead of `test_value`.

Medium

 

 

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Carrier View - CDS Data Model'
@Search.searchable: true
define view entity ZTest
  as selectfrom /dmo/flight as Flight
  association [1] to /DMO/I_Carrier as _Airline 
{
      @UI.lineItem: [ { position: 10, label: 'Airline'} ]
      @Search.defaultSearchElement: true
      @Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.7
      @ObjectModel.text.association: '_Airline'
  key Flight.carrier_id     as AirlineID,
      @UI.lineItem: [ { position: 20, label: 'Connection Number' } ]
  key Flight.connection_id  as ConnectionID,
      @UI.lineItem: [ { position: 30, label: 'Flight Date' } ]
  key Flight.flight_date    as FlightDate,
      @UI.lineItem: [ { position: 40, label: 'Price' } ]
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      Flight.price          as Price,
      Flight.currency_code  as CurrencyCode,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      case
       when price <= 1000 or price is initial
        then cast(price as /dmo/flight_price preserving type)
       else
         cast( 1000 + (cast(price as abap.numc(16,2)) - 1000 ) * 
division(9,10,2) as /dmo/flight_price)
       end as

  /* Associations */
      _Airline
}

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 5, `select` from written as `selectfrom`.
Bug 2: At line 6, association statement is incomplete, should have `on $projection.AirlineID = _Airline.AirlineID` after  `_Airline`.
Bug 3: At line 28, `end as` Variable Name missing and comma (,) missing after Variable name.
Bug 4: At line 26, `abap.numc` is a wrong data type, it should be `abap.dec`.

Hard

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@abapcatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZTESt1'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: '#NOT_REQUIRED'
@EndUserText.label: 'Test'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
   serviceQuality: #X,
   sizeCategory: #S,
   dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ZTest!
  with parameters
    P_Currency :/dmo/currency_code
  as select from /dmo/travel
  association [0] to /DMO/I_Overall_Status_VH_Text as _Status on $projection.Status = _Status.OverallStatus
{
  key travel_id                 as TravelId,
      description               as Description,
      total_price               as TotalPrice,
      currency_code             as CurrencyCode,
      concat_with_space(cast(total_price as abap.char( 20) ),
      $parameters.P_Curency,1) as AmountInCurrency,
      @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ element: { name:
      '/DMO/I_Overall_Status_VH',entity: 'OverallStatus' } }]
      status                    as Status,
      _Status.Text              as OverallStatus,
}

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 21, name of view entity should be corrected to `ZTest`.
Bug 2: At line 3, unwanted `‘ ‘` for `#NOT_REQUIRED`.
Bug 3: At line 26, unwanted `,` after `OverallStatus`. 
Bug 4: At line 15, association cardinality should be [1] instead of [0].
Bug 5: At line 23, in Syntax of `@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition` `element` instead of `entity` written.
Bug 6: At line 24, in Syntax of `@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition` `entity` instead of `element` written.
Bug 7: At line 22, in the syntax of `concat_with_space` there is a type at `$parameters.P_Curency,1)`.
Bug 8: At line 19, missing annotation for `Total_price` field.
Bug 9: At line 2, `@abapcatalog.sqlViewName` is not allowed in view entities. 

Bash

Easy

 

 

 

#!/usr/bin/env bash

read -p 'Enter your name: '
$answer = $REPLY
echo 'Thank you. You entered $answer.'

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 4, in the assignment of `$REPLY` to `answer`, `answer` should not be prefixed with `$`. (this is not Perl!)
Bug 2: At line 4, in the assignment, no spaces are allowed either before or after the equals symbol `=`.
Bug 3: At line 5, inside single quotes, variables like `$answer` are not expanded (double quotes are needed here).

Medium

 

 

 

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -e
set pipefail

main {

  local account="${1:?No account specified}"

  # Cause script to abend if no account data stored
  pass "btp/$account" > dev/null

  cf login
    -a "$(pass "btp/$account/cfapiendpoint")" \
    -u "$(pass "btp/$account/user")" \
    -p "$(pass "btp/$account/password")"

  echo <<EOF
Thank you.
Logged in successfully.
EOF

}

main "$@"

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 4, `pipefail` is an option that you need to set using the `-o` option of `set`.
Bug 2: At line 6, functions can be declared with the `function` keyword like this: `function main { ... }` or without the `function` keyword (as here), but parentheses are needed before the code block, like this: `main () { ... }`.
Bug 3: At line 11, `dev/null` is relative, not absolute (i.e. there's no leading `/`) so this is not really what you want (to cleanly suppress `STDOUT`, output it must be sent to `/dev/null`).
Bug 4: At line 13, the newline at the end of `cf login` is not escaped (with a `\`) so this `cf` command execution is not valid.
Bug 5: At line 18, echo does not read from STDIN so we can't use a heredoc (<<); instead, `cat` could be used (or `printf`).

Hard

 

 

 

#!/usr/bin/env bash

read -p 'Enter a color: ' color

if [ $color == 'black' ]; then
    echo "Black is not a color, it is the absence of color"
fi

if [ $color =~ ^(red|green|blue)$ ]; then
    echo "You chose a primary color"
fi

cd colors
touch "$color.txt"

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 3, using `read` without `-r` will mangle backslashes (shellcheck Informational).
Bug 2: At line 5 & 9, it's usually a very bad idea to not quote your variables, especially in a `[` construct.
Bug 3: At line 9, to use the regular expression operator `=~` you need the Bash-specific `[[ ... ]]` construct, and not the `[` command - see https://mywiki.wooledge.org/BashFAQ/031 and The open square bracket [ is an executable.
Bug 4: At line 13, not checking the success (or otherwise) of a `cd` command is dangerous, and could result in the script creating, changing or deleting files in a place you didn't expect!

jq

Easy

We want a simple list of products and the categories they're in. The output should look like this:
Chai is in the Beverages category
Chang is in the Beverages category
Aniseed Syrup is in the Condiments category
Chef Anton's Cajun Seasoning is in the Condiments category
...

The invocation of the jq script (including bugs) to produce this looks like this:

 

 

 

jq --raw \
  '.()|"(.ProductName) is in the \(Category.CategoryName) category" \
  products.json

 

 

 

and on its own, with extra whitespace, the (buggy) jq filter looks like this:

 

 

 

.()
| "(.ProductName) is in the \(Category.CategoryName) category"

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 1 in the jq filter, the array iterator is `[]`, not `()` (Array Object Value Iterator).
Bug 2: At line 2 of the jq filter, the string interpolation construct is `\( ... )` so there's a backslash missing in the first instance (String Interpolation)
Bug 3: At line 2 of the jq filter, in the second instance of an expression being inserted into the string, the identity filter `.` is missing - it should be `.Category.CategoryName` (Identity)

The correct filter should be:
.[]|"\(.ProductName) is in the \(.Category.CategoryName) category"

Medium

We want to know how many products are still being sold, i.e. not discontinued. The answer should be a simple numeric value, like this (8 of the 77 total products in this data set are discontinued, so the value here is correct):
69

The invocation of the jq script (including bugs) to produce this looks like this:

 

 

 

jq --raw \
  'select(.Discontinued)|length' \
  products.json​

 

 

 

and on its own, with extra whitespace, the (buggy) jq filter looks like this:

 

 

 

select(.Discontinued)
| length

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 1 of the jq filter, the outermost element in the data set is an array, so we must use map to iterate over and call `select` (Map).
Bug 2: At line 2 of the jq filter, we're after a count of the products that are not discontinued, so we need to negate the boolean expression inside the select (Select).

The correct filter should be:
map(select(.Discontinued|not))|length

Hard

Based on the data set, we want a summary of categories with the total number of units of stock for each of those categories. The output should look like this:

 

 

 

{
  "Beverages": 559,
  "Condiments": 507,
  "Confections": 386,
  "Dairy Products": 393,
  "Grains/Cereals": 308,
  "Meat/Poultry": 165,
  "Produce": 100,
  "Seafood": 701
}

 

 

 

The invocation of the jq script (including bugs) to produce this looks like this:

 

 

 

jq --raw \
  'group(.Category.CategoryID)|map({first|Category.CategoryName:map(.UnitsInStock)|+})|sum' \
  products.json

 

 

 

and on its own, with extra whitespace, the (buggy) jq filter looks like this:

 

 

 

group(.Category.CategoryID)
| map({
    first|Category.CategoryName:
    map(.UnitsInStock)
    | +
  })
| sum

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 1 of the jq filter, the `group` function, which takes a path expression, is `group_by`, not `group` (Group By).
Bug 2: At line 3 of the jq filter, while `first` (which is just syntactic sugar for .[0] (Built Ins - first)) can be followed by a pipe, it can also be followed directly by the identity filter, which is missing here (should be `first.Category.CategoryName`, or `first|.Category.CategoryName` but not `first|Category.CategoryName`) (First Last Nth).
Bug 3: At line 3 of the jq filter, to use an expression as a key in a property literal, you need to wrap it in parentheses, i.e. `{(first.Category.CategoryName): ... }`, not `{first.Category.CategoryName: ...}`.
Bug 4: At line 5 of the jq filter, while `+` is valid in jq (it's an operator), it's not valid in the position it is here; the `add` filter is needed to add values in an array (Add).
Bug 5: At line 7 of the jq filter, `sum` is not a valid filter in jq, it's `add`.

The correct filter should be:
group_by(.Category.CategoryID)
| map({
    (first.Category.CategoryName):
    map(.UnitsInStock)
    | add
  })
| add

JavaScript

Easy

 

 

 

const bugFunc = (areThereBugs) -> {
  if (areThereBags) {
    console.log('Quick! Call an exterminator!);
  } else {
    console.log('Are you sure there aren't any bugs?');
  } else {
    console.log('No bugs here! Thank god.');
  }
}

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 1, arrow function syntax is wrong. It should be `=>`, not `->`.
Bug 2: At line 2, variable `areThereBugs` is misspelled to `areThereBags`. 
Bug 3: At line 3, There is a missing quote at the end of the string in first console.log.
Bug 4: At line 5, The `'` in `aren't` hasn't been escaped in the second console.log.
Bug 5: At line 6, There's an extra `else` that will never be called. To fix this, line 4 should either be an `else if` or the second `else` should be removed.

Medium

 

 

 

for (let i = 0: i < 5: i++) {
  console.log(looped {i} times);
}

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 1, for loop condition is using `:` instead of `;` between parameters.
Bug 2: At line 2, in the console.log statement, there are missing quotes. The correct quotes to be used are backticks `\`` for the template literal so the `i` variable is correctly updated within the string.
Bug 3: At line 2, in the console.log statement, there is a missing `$` in front of the variable curly braces `{}`.

Hard

For the last JavaScript bug hunt, "bug" identification is the name of the game. What most people call bugs or insects are actually several different classifications of the phylum arthropods.

The goal of the function in this bug hunt is to take in an object of an unidentified "bug" and determine if it is a part of the class Insecta or Arachnids, and if Insecta, further identify if it is a true bug in the order Hemiptera. To make things simple, the function should return one of the three values, "Arachnid", "Insect", or "True Bug".

Please note that some of this data might not technically be correct, entomology wise. For example, there are some arachnids with less than 8 legs. This does not count as a bug. To simplify things, the objects below are going to be used as the source of truth for arthropod classification. They should be used to determine if the function `isArachnidInsectOrBug` will return the correct output.

Arachnid

 

 

 

{
    antennae: false,
    wingPairs: false,
    bodySegments: 2,
    legs: 8
}

 

 

 

Insect

 

 

 

{
    antennae: true,
    wingPairs: [0, 2],
    mouthType: [
      "sponging",
      "siphoning",
      "chewing",
      "piercing/sucking flexible",
      "piercing/sucking rigid"
    ],
    bodySegments: 3,
    legs: 6
}

 

 

 

True Bug

 

 

 

{
    antennae: true,
    wingPairs: [0, 2],
    mouthType: "piercing/sucking rigid",
    bodySegments: 3,
    legs: 6
}

 

 

 

Hint: Keep these classification objects in mind when looking at the functions in the bug hunt.
Below are examples of potential input to the function `IsArachnidInsectOrBug`. In order to determine what is what, they need to match the values in the classification objects above. Keep in mind that all true bugs are insects but not all insects are true bugs and arachnids are neither. No coding bugs will be about the data itself being wrong.
Assume only one value in the classification object parameters with arrays will be chosen for the input object. For example, in Insect, `wingPairs` would equal either `0` or `2`, not `[0]` or `[0, 2]`. You can see this in input example 2.
Example of what could be sent into the function and expected output:
Arthropod Input Example 1

 

 

 

{
    antennae: false,
    wingPairs : false,
    bodySegments: 2,
    legs: 8
}

 

 

 

Expected Output: "Arachnid"
Arthropod Input Example 2

 

 

 

{
    antennae: false,
    wingPairs : false,
    mouthType: "piercing/sucking flexible",
    bodySegments: 3,
    legs: 6
}

 

 

 

Expected Output: "Insect"
Remember, best practices is not a justification for a bug. If the code will run and output the expected result, it does not count as a bug. Also keep in mind that the point is to count the bugs, not how to fix the bugs. One fix might take care of several bugs but should not be counted as only one bug.
These are complex bugs so look carefully.
Find the bugs in the following code:

 

 

 

function isArachnidInsectOrBug (arthropod) {
    let bugType = isInsectOrBug(arthropod);

    if (!bugType) {
        return isArachnid(arthropod);
    }
}

function isInsectOrBug(arthropod) {
    let bugType = "";
    switch (arthropod.mouthType) {
        case "sponging":
        case "siphoning":
        case "chewing":
        case "piercing/sucking flexible": {
            bugType = "Insect";
        }
        case "piercing/sucking rigid": {
            bugType = "True Bug";
        }
    }
}

function isArachnid ({antennae, wingPairs, bodySegments, legs}) {
    if (!antennae || !wingPairs || bodySegments === 2 || legs ===  {
        return true;
    }
}

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 2, calling `isInsectOrBug` first will throw an error if the arthropod is an Arachnid as the arachnid object does not have the `mouthType` parameter. To fix this, call `isArachnid` first and return `Arachnid` before checking if the arthropod is an insect or true bug.
Bug 2: At line 16, in the switch statement in `isInsectOrBug`, doesn't have a break statement and will always cause `bugType` to be "True Bug".
Bug 3: At line 9, `isInsectOrBug` does not have a return statement.
Bug 4: At line 25, `isArachnid` is using the wrong boolean operators to test if arthropods is an arachnid. If any of those are correct, the function will return true. This will cause certain insects to return as Arachnid and is not correct.
Bug 5: At line 27, `isArachnid` does not return anything if bug isn't an arachnid. This could cause an error as the returned value will be null. This could be handled in the `isArachnidInsectOrBug` due to truthy values but can cause unexpected errors so returning false is the best option.
Bug 6: At line 5, `isArachnidInsectOrBug` is expecting isArachnid to return the string "Arachnid" but is instead getting a boolean. This will cause isArachnidInsectOrBug to return a boolean rather than one of the three expected strings. To fix this, `isArachnidInsectOrBug` should use the value to return either "Arachnid" or further check for insect or true bug.
Bug 7: At line 5, `isArachnidInsectOrBug` will only return if bug is an arachnid. It should return the results of `isInsectOrBug` if not an empty string and otherwise throw an error if insect is none of the three expected results.

Without bugs, this is what the function could look like (without accounting for best practices and shortening code):
function isArachnidInsectOrBug (arthropod) {
    let bugType = "";

    if (isArachnid(arthropod)) {
        bugType = "Arachnid";
    } else {
        bugType = isInsectOrBug(arthropod);
    }

    if (bugType !== "") {
        return bugType;
    } else {
        throw new Error({
            arthropod: arthropod,
            err: "Arthropod is not an Arachnid, Insect, or True Bug."
        })
    }
}

function isInsectOrBug(arthropod) {
    let bugType = "";
    switch (arthropod.mouthType) {
        case "sponging":
        case "siphoning":
        case "chewing":
        case "piercing/sucking flexible": {
            // Alternatively, the string could be returned here instead of using the break statement and the bugType variable.
            bugType = "Insect";
            break;
        }
        case "piercing/sucking rigid": {
            bugType = "True Bug";
            break;
        }
    }
    return bugType;
}

function isArachnid ({antennae, wingPairs, bodySegments, legs}) {
    // Alternatively, the result of the boolean statement in the if statement could be returned directly.
    // EX. `return (!antennae && !wingPairs && bodySegments === 2 && legs === 8);`
    if (!antennae && !wingPairs && bodySegments === 2 && legs ===  {
        return true;
    } else {
        return false;
    }
}

Python

Easy

 

 

 

def hello_world();
print "Hello, World!"

hello_world()

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 1, `;` should be `:`.
Bug 2: At line 2, the print statement should be indented after the function definition on line 1.
Bug 3: At line 2, The string "Hello, World!" should be wrapped in parentheses in call to `print`.

Medium

 

 

 

from typing import Final

#Store integers 1, 2 and 3 in variables `ONE`, `TWO`, `THREE`
ONE, TWO, THREE = range(1, 3)
ZERO : Final = 0

def check_number (x : int) -> str:
    if x <= ONE:
        result='smaller than or equal to 1'
    elif x = TWO:
        result='equal to 2'
    then:
        result='equal to or greater than 3'

    returns result

a=input("Input the integer number: ")

print(f"{a} is {check_number(a)}")

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 10, boolean operator '==' should be used instead of '='. 
Bug 2: At line 12, `then` should be `else`.
Bug 3: At line 15, there is an extra `s` on `returns`. It should be `return result`.
Bug 4: At line 4, `range(1, 3)` will only provide values `1` and `2`, which is not enough values for the number of variables provided. Instead, it should be `range(1, 4)`. 
Bug 5: At line 8 and 17, `<=` is not supported between instances of `str` (`a`) and `int` (`x`). To fix this, on line 17, it should be `a=int(input("Input the integer number: "))` to convert a string to an integer.

Hard

 

 

 

from dataclass import dataclass

class PointInTime:
    year : int
    month: int
    day  : int

point = PointInTime(1974, 12, 23)
print(point)  # Output should be: PointInTime(year=1974, month=12, day=23)

 

 

 

Intended bugs:

Spoiler
Bug 1: At line 1, there is no module named 'dataclass'. Instead, it should be `from dataclasses import dataclass`.
Bug 2: At line 2, before the class `PointInTime`, there needs to be a decorator, `@dataClass` as `PointInTime()` takes no arguments without the decorator.

Wrap Up

I hope everyone had fun. Let us know in the comments if you enjoyed this and want to bring it back next year. Also feel free to share bugs you found that you were proud of or bugs you struggled with. 

While Bugtoberfest might be over, Devtoberfest isn't and there's one more week of content, and another Fun Friday event left!

entry.title: Re: Capitalization option in A/P invoice
entry.content:
Hi,

If you create an AP Invoice for fixed asset item, then capitalization will be created for the same. The FA item is not a normal item which will create records based like this. It either needs to be capitalized or when the same is purchased via the AP Invoice, then the capitalization starts at that time.

If you create subsequent AP Invoice for FA item, then it will be considered as sub-acquisition and accordingly added in the FA value for depreciation.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Outbound connection to SAP S/4 system from BTP
entry.content:
Yes, you can implement OAuth 2.0 authentication while integrating SAP BTP with an SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system as a destination system.

Here's how you can set it up:

  1. SAP Cloud Connector: You need to configure the SAP Cloud Connector (SCC) to enable secure communication between SAP BTP and your on-premise S/4HANA system.

  2. OAuth Token Service: SAP BTP supports OAuth 2.0 as an authentication mechanism. You will need to configure your SAP S/4HANA system as an OAuth 2.0 client to consume tokens from an OAuth token service, such as SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) or any other compatible OAuth provider.

  3. Destination Configuration in BTP: In SAP BTP, you can configure a destination that uses OAuth as the authentication method. You will need to set up the destination in SAP BTP’s connectivity service, providing the OAuth token service URL, client ID, and secret, and choosing OAuth2ClientCredentials or OAuth2Password as the authentication method depending on your use case.

  4. Security Configuration: Ensure that the OAuth 2.0 authentication method is enabled and configured on the SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system as well, using tools such as the SAP Gateway.

In summary, OAuth 2.0 is supported, and the integration can be achieved by configuring both the BTP destination and your SAP S/4HANA system, along with SAP Cloud Connector for secure communication.

entry.title: multiple different articles in same case for purchase
entry.content:
I need to group assorted articles into the same Casepack.  I cannot do this as a prepack using variants..

My company runs Restautant/food service sites in airports.  Example:  we sell Bagels.  In one airport we purchase a group of assorted bagel flavors as a prepack (12 plain, 6 onion, 6 raisin), however in another airport that prepack comes from a different vendor and it is (12 Plain, 6 onion, 6 raisin, 6 poppyseed), then in another airport we purchase from 3rd vendor all individually.  

I cannot utilize Prepack due to all bagel flavors below would be variants, and since the combination is different based on the vendor it won't work. One solution would be creating a separate prepack set up for each vendor, but the result would be having 45 different article numbers for "Plain Bagel"

Is there a way to group "single" articles into a case-pack for purchase order processing.  The vendor in the above scenario invoices us for "Assorted Bagels", if we enter each bagel flavor separately on the PO, we won't be able to execute automatic 3-way match and invoice will have to be manually released.

entry.title: Re: IGS Watchdog could not be started
entry.content:
Hi Manas,

check if the igs process is in running state. Because process gets started based on two factors, system will execute associated kernel file and read the parameters from instance profile.

 

In my case, IGS was running was running fine on 01 node whereas 02 had issue And, this was because of the below parameter set in profile of 02 instance.

*************************************************************************************

#Start_Program_01 = local $(_IG) -mode=profile pf=$(_PF)
# Change IGS
Start_Program_02 = local $(_IG) -mode=profile pf=$(DIR_PROFILE)$(DIR_SEP)<SID>_D01_<hostname> --> Here the instance profile of 02 was pointing to D01 instance profile.

*************************************************************************************

Fix: I corrected the profile name for D02 and restarted D02 instance.

Please write back if you have any questions.

Thanks.

We have Solman 7.2 implemented and I access it using the Fiori dashboard. About a week and a half ago I started getting an error when trying to access Solman. I click on the "Solution Documentation" tile. The system tries to open Solman, but instead displays:

500 Internal Server Error Exception CXC_SMUD_ASSERT_D occurred because of error: SMUD_ASSERT_FAIL_ERR. Server time: [blank]

I can't find any OSS notes about this. I've contacted our system administrator. He says it's a template error, but he doesn't know how to fix it and says he must put in a ticket to SAP. I have no confidence that they will respond quickly. 

As far as I know, no one else on my team is having this problem. I'm getting the same result in Edge, Chrome, and Firefox, so I don't think it's browser cache or anything similar, but it has to be specific to my ID somehow.

Can anyone offer any insight? Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Activating Automatic sap* user
entry.content:

entry.title: How to Block material for plant maintenance (asset management) in S/4HANA Public Cloud ?
entry.content:
We need to know how to block a material so that it cannot be used in plant maintenance management (asset management): creation of maintenance orders, maintenance advices, confirmations, etc.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Block material for plant maintenance (asset ma...
entry.content:
The field Cross-Plant Material Status found in the Product Master can be used to Block a material from being used in Asset Management 

 

The field Field Cross-Plant Material Status and its Configuration Activity ( Define Material Statuses 102618 ) effects the following application areas :

•Materials management (for example, in purchasing and in inventory management)

•Production planning and control

•Plant maintenance

•Warehouse management

•Costing with a quantity structure

entry.title: Re: Why i can insert records into standart table?
entry.content: Just wondering: why would you ever even want to directly insert an entry into a SAP standard table like MARA? Custom code should never directly update SAP tables but should always rely on SAP-provided methods to do that like e.g. BAPIs in order to ensure data integrity and proper logging. The entry your code inserted will be completely useless and you can do it exactly once. So, what's the purpose?

entry.title: Re: Legacy Learning page - Deprecation and Adoption of New Learning experience timeline

Last I heard, yes the legacy page will be removed with 1H 2025 release.

entry.title: SAP TM Strategy to Send Notification E-mail Carrier and Person Responsible
entry.content:
SAP TM Strategy to Send Notification E-mail Carrier and Person Responsible

Follow up the previous Blogs for the Alert notification (Set-up Alert Management in SAP Transportation Mana... - SAP Community), in this blog will explain how to Send Notification E-mail Carrier and Person Responsible in the Freight Document.

This step will send email to the Carrier or Person Responsible in the FO in case i.e item quantity changes.

Follow this step-in order to properly set up the strategy creation, you can find it in SPRO à SAP Transportation Management -> Basic Functions-> Process Controller for all the steps below.

  1. Define methods:

Method: NTFN_MAIL
Service: TOR_CHACO
Description: Description text
Method Type: Basic Method
Class/Interface: /SCMTMS/CL_CHACO_METHODS
Interface Component: SEND_NOTIFICATION_MAIL

  1. Define Strategy:

Strategy: NTFN_S
Service: TOR_CHACO
Description: SEND_NOTIFICATION_MAIL

  1. Assign Methods to a Strategy:

Strategy: NTFN_S
Method: NTFN_MAIL
Sequence: 10
Description: SEND_NOTIFICATION_MAIL 

  1.  Define Parameters

Parameter: NOTIFY_CAR
Service: TOR_CHACO
Param. Type: Logical Value
Data Element: BOOLE_D

  1. Define Parameters

Parameter: MAIL_SNDR
Service: TOR_CHACO
Param. Type: Character String
Data Element: /SCMTMS/UI_EMAIL

  1. Assign Parameters to a Method

Method: NTFN_MAIL
Parameter Name: NOTIFY_CAR

Method: NTFN_MAIL
Parameter Name: MAIL_SNDR

  1. Assign Method Parameters to a Strategy

Strategy: NTFN_S
Method: NTFN_MAIL
Parameter Name: NOTIFY_CAR
Param. Type: Logical Value
Number:
Text Field: X

Strategy: NTFN_S
Method: NTFN_MAIL
Parameter Name: MAIL_SNDR
Param. Type: Character String
Number:
Text Field:

After creating it assign the strategy to your Document Type direct or via Condition:

Is very important the Email will only send out to Carrier if the Freight Document have the   Subcontracting Status (Send to Carrier or Changed After Sending)

The email to the Person Responsible in the Freight Document will only consider if the change does not belong to the same.

If you want to check more technical the criteria you can see in this class /SCMTMS/CL_CHACO_NOTIFICATION - GROUPED_NOTIFY Line 81 to 133.

Now let’s give a try in the FO which I have remove one Sales Order after planning.

FO is Send to Carrier:

 

After remove Sales order 100484 the message is trigger

The email is sent and you can open direct the Freight order via Hyper Link.

Hope this help you!

Thank you and regards.

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: PaPM Universal Model: Parameter
entry.content:
Hi,

As you mentioned, there are no parameters or selection fields by process. However, what you can do is transfer the first activity's parameter/selection to the following activities. You can do this in the General Information of the first activity. Therefore, you only need to set the parameter for the first activity, and the parameters will be transferred to the subsequent activities.

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: QM Bapi add a characteristic result for an inspection lot
entry.content:
I think you will find documentation to help you in the sample code for function module EXIT_SAPLQEEM_021 in enhancement QEEM0021 in transaction SMOD.

This documentation points you to sample function module QINT_EXAMPLE_USER_EXIT in include file LXQEEF14.

This example is designed for the user defined upload buttons in transaction QE51N. It shows how you can (1)  read the detailed information for any characteristic of any operation of any inspection lot, (2) modify it, (3) write the changes back to the system.

entry.title: Re: Accessing HANA/ECC without BAPIs and Gateway?
entry.content:
> I'm still unsure of the best method to access my ECC database information.

I think you are trying to do the wrong thing... An ECC is NOT a database, it provides "business functionality", which includes much more than simple "data storage and search/query functionality": from sanity checks on the "plausibility" or logical integrity of business data to authorization checks to make sure unqualified persons cannot see data that is not meant for them... So you should not regard your ECC system as simply a "database", (in which case you could simply get an ODBC driver and access the database below the ECC system, but this is not to be recommended...)

Nevertheless, the answer to your question is "yes".
(But I guess, this is not the answer you are looking for... 🙂 The problem is, that your question is too general. A typical ECC system has thousands (maybe millions?) of interfaces, some RFC-based, some SOAP/WebService-based, some REST-Service based, others IDoc/EDI-flatfile based, etc... No one from the low level technical groups like "ABAP", "Cloud Connector" will be able to give you a meaningful answer here. First you need to find out, which data/information you want -- e.g. product descriptions from the Materials Management application or invoice details from the purchase orders of your customers -- and then label your question with the corresponding application (like SAP MM) to get the attention of the experts from that area. They will be able to tell you, which interfaces this application provides. Some applications may only have exposed ALE or RFC interfaces, others only SOAP or REST interfaces, and others both types, in order to be callable from "both worlds" (the RFC world and the HTTP world).)
